name: SSH Brute Force Attempt
pattern: Failed password for .* from .* port \d+ ssh
description: Multiple failed SSH authentication attempts detected, indicating a possible brute-force attack against the SSH service.

name: Sudo Command Abuse
description: A privileged command was executed via sudo. Frequent or unexpected sudo usage may indicate privilege escalation attempts.
mitre_technique: T1548.003 - Abuse Elevation Control Mechanism: Sudo and Sudo Caching

name: New User Account Created
description: A new user account has been created on the system. Unauthorized account creation is a common persistence technique.

name: Crontab Modification Detected
description: A crontab file has been modified. Attackers frequently modify cron jobs to establish persistent scheduled execution.

name: SUID Binary Modification
description: A binary in a system path has had its SUID bit set. SUID binaries can be exploited for privilege escalation.
mitre_technique: T1548.001 - Abuse Elevation Control Mechanism: Setuid and Setgid

name: Log File Clearing
description: An attempt to clear or truncate system log files was detected. Log clearing is a common defense evasion tactic.
mitre_technique: T1070.002 - Indicator Removal on Host: Clear Linux or Mac System Logs

name: Kernel Module Loaded
description: A kernel module has been loaded. Malicious kernel modules (rootkits) can hide processes, files, and network connections.

name: Reverse Shell Indicator
description: A command pattern consistent with a reverse shell was detected. This may indicate active exploitation of the host.
mitre_technique: T1059.004 - Command and Scripting Interpreter: Unix Shell

name: Suspicious File Download
description: A file download command was executed. Attackers often download tools or payloads onto compromised systems.

name: Privilege Escalation via Su/Sudo
description: An attempt to switch to the root user was detected. This may indicate privilege escalation activity.

name: Password File Access Attempt
description: Access to the system password or shadow file was detected. This is a strong indicator of credential harvesting.

name: Netcat Listener Detected
description: A netcat listener was detected on the system. Netcat listeners are commonly used as part of bind shells or data exfiltration channels.
